item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So this is a scip I think suffers from 'not enough' syndrome. It's not got enough details and anomalous nature to be considered a serious scip (as others stated it borders on mundane, deliberate sabotage with the only 'anomaly' being transmission). It also doesn't have enough humour to be a -J. That and the horrible tacked on CoD reference drops this to a -1 from me.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

Reading this SCP reminded me of another SCP based off scantron tests, which is CWW's 2496. This SCP affects the machines that grade the tests, and that one affects the test itself.

Proposing adding the following line to the Containment Procedures:

Usage of SCP-1292 instances to grade copies of SCP-2496 is forbidden.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes I know, necropost and all. But this thread is the best. I just wanted all of you to know that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Do you care to explain these terrible grades?"
"Yes, it was the scantron disease"
*Foundation Agent bursts through the door and tackles the scantron machine*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow hot damn son
please no.

(spoiler) +1 (/spoiler)

edit^: woops oh well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who infected my scantron with slacker? I WILL END YOU! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't joke about things like this. Now, to outsmart the Scantron anomaly, I have to choose random questions and put down wrong answers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: by 5 months! You should've joined much earlier

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was all ready to make a Scantron joke and then you come and ruin everything

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well this certainly promotes how much hair tearing, desk banging, pen breaking, pencil chewing, eraser marking, test taking I am in right now!

+1 you clever bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too close to a J for me, but not funny enough. Sorry :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Call of Duty reference kind of killed it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am the definition of neutral.
I'm going to chalk it up to the fact that I haven't had to take a test, scantron or otherwise, in twenty years so test anxiety is not something I can relate to anymore.
I'd probably edge over into an up-vote if there was anything else going on to grab my interest, maybe make it a machine-meme passed from device to device when incorrectly scored sheets are re-checked after a "malfunction"?

Also, the first bulletpoint is awkwardly worded and I think it hurts the tone. How about "20-25% answers are randomly marked incorrect"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll throw my hat in on the opinion that if this wasn't tossed in during a finals week it'd have already gotten deleted. IMO it needs to get more meat to stay a mainlist SCP, or just needs to accept its COD destiny and go be a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just doesn't quite crack anomalous, for me. I'm also a little iffy on the details. If the SCP is a "fault" it seems a little tricky to detect/contain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting idea… Needs fleshed out more, though. No vote for now. Somehow I got the impression of a stressed out, fratboy technopath from this article, instead of a machine itself "channelling" the anxiety.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goooootchaaa. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup, that's why it's called a "personal opinion."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was neutral until the COD thing. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think implying you are a Scantron machine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ­>implying I don't ace tests by my very nature

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this idea (and think it's taken seriously enough for the mainlist) but it doesn't feel very substantive. This could go to other, more interesting places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bombed hard and gone to play COD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was before my time, but the story as I understand it is that someone asked why the Foundation didn't supply 682 with babies to calm it since it was so sedate when exposed to that one child, SCP-053. Rather that explaining that that was dumb, Clef wrote it all up to be in the list including the throwing of the "guest researcher" into the containment chamber.

Not that knowing the full story would really affect your opinion on those logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You would know, wouldn't you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the impression I got from this as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My headcanon: It's the kids. The scantron machines aren't thinking that they want to play Call of Duty, they're just channeling the sad, hopeless thoughts of the kids who marked the tests they're processing. With all the hopelessness, intensity, and pressure people feel while taking an important test, that stuff builds up, and here, it eventually got into the scantron machines and made them "mediums" (Like a spirit medium channels ghosts) for the kids who fail these things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem, I like getting replies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This either needs a rewrite into a -J, or needs to be fleshed out and stay mainlist. I like the occasional really simple SCP, but the concept here isn't interesting enough to fly solo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn't supposed to be funny. It's a scantron machine that comes down with a case of test-checking anxiety.

There's nothing goddamn funny about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't forget the silly experiment logs. For some reason, SCP-682's log once had a Foundation researcher throw children in because the guy wanted to be funny or something. The next log had another doctor throw him in. I'm not sure if that bit is still in, but it gives me exactly the sort of feeling "humorous" addenda do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was a time period that everything became really silly, with little notes all over the place that would indicate the place was ran by blithering incompetents and people with the impulse control of toddlers. Merely being funny (or attempting to be) isn't really enough to make something lolfoundation. It's got to go that extra mile of making the place seem like a frathouse or pre-school.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed it, but found myself laughing (and relating to the addendum quite frankly…). I upvoted because I enjoyed it, and the tone. I think some more humor should be put in to make it a J, or take some humor out to be a normal SCP.

Also this might be just me, but the line
"the fault has been found to spread between scanners connected on the same network, electrical circuit, or directly"
felt a little awkward to me,
"the fault has been found to spread between scanners connected directly, via electrical circuit, or on the same network"
may flow a little better, but this is a personal opinion.
My reason being the original ending with "directly" feels like it begs the question "Directly to what?"
Or is this just me?

Edit; once again i managed to hit the "reply" instead of "new post." My apoliges Dr Ivo…I'm rather tired : (

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought it extended to 'humorous' quotes/transcripts as well.

I don't hate the concept, but think the addenda is a little lazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't really funny enough to be a -J or elaborate enough to be a mainlister. I'd suggest toning it up a little either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the record, LOLFoundation as I understand it:

Useless addendums and/or tacked on researcher's notes that make the Foundation seem inept for the purpose of pointless and ineffectve humor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's funny so it must be a -J!

Whatever you say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But ossifer, it isn't bad lolfoundation!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You keep using that word

I don't think you know what it means

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my personal and very humble opinion, this is a -J. I understand and empathize with the idea, but downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. I laughed plenty, but didn't feel there was quite enough "meat" to upvote. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It isn't even LOLFoundation tho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sorry, transcript killed it for me. bad lolfoundation…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is… neat, but I'm also left going, "Okay, what's the big deal?" I mean, yes, it's bad, but it's also just…

Okay, I guess what I'm trying to say is "It needs a hook".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It would have bombed hard.

Still upvoting, because I like the idea and am hoping it'll get fleshed out a little soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It makes me wonder how this would have been viewed if it had been posted at any time other than a Finals Week.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: don't tell me what to say bro

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As it's finals week, this makes me want to weep with fear. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have no power over me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If what the Scantron said were to be tweaked a little, this could be fairly awesome. Give em test anxiety, make em freak the hell out. That would be cool as hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bravo. +1 Testing Nervousness

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As relevant as this is to my current state, I don't think it has enough of a hook here. Certainly not enough to remain as a main-list.

item.title: I see EVERYTHING
item.content:encoded: "A whole new wooooorrrrrrllllllllldd. A new fantastic point of view. No one to tell me no, or where to go, or say I'm only dreeeeemmminAWholeNeeewww woorrrrrddddd"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel this could be much better if it were more subtle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My feet just ain't tapping to the beat of this number, sonny. Gotta take you down a peg.
Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this works as a mainlist article. The skip isn't serious, but the article is.

It seems kinda incomplete to me, though. It makes Scantrons act like lazy teens, good concept. Funny. But that's it. That's everything. There's no intrigue. It seems like it could go further, somehow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not

Uh

I'm not joking around?

Fuck I'm no good at puns. Does that even count as one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have actually, but I understand what you're getting at. I meant that it would mark letters that corresponded to dots, and would form a diagonal line if filled in. I'll change it to clarify that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get it, but…

+ show
- hide

If you've never used a Scantron grader yourself, there's no reason you would know this. The machines don't actually print in the bubbles. They just print a block letter "A", "B", or whatever next to the bubbles, and they only mark incorrect answers. So the machine couldn't really make diagonal lines or patterns in the bubbles. Sorry, I know that kind of spoils the joke. I guess you can invoke artistic license if you want to keep it that way. : )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's wrong, Rumetzen? You seem a bit -Jded.
